randomNumber:
' VBScript Random Number Generation

' Seed the random number generator
Randomize

' Generate a random float between 0 and 1
Dim randomFloat
randomFloat = Rnd()
WScript.Echo "Random Float [0-1): " & randomFloat

' Generate a random integer in a range [min, max]
Dim min, max, randomInt
min = 1
max = 100
randomInt = Int((max - min + 1) * Rnd + min)
WScript.Echo "Random Integer [" & min & "-" & max & "]: " & randomInt

' Select a random element from an array
Dim fruits, randomIndex, randomFruit
fruits = Array("apple", "banana", "cherry")
If UBound(fruits) >= 0 Then
    randomIndex = Int(UBound(fruits) + 1 * Rnd)
    randomFruit = fruits(randomIndex)
    WScript.Echo "Random fruit: " & randomFruit
End If
reverseString:
' Use the built-in StrReverse function

Dim original, reversed
original = "Hello, VBScript!"
reversed = StrReverse(original)

WScript.Echo "Original: " & original
WScript.Echo "Reversed: " & reversed

' StrReverse is generally safe for the character sets VBScript supports
Dim originalUnicode
originalUnicode = "VBScript Example"
WScript.Echo "Original Unicode: " & originalUnicode
WScript.Echo "Reversed Unicode: " & StrReverse(originalUnicode)
fibonacci:
' Function to calculate the Nth Fibonacci number iteratively
Function Fibonacci(n)
    If Not IsNumeric(n) Or n < 0 Then
        Fibonacci = -1 ' Indicate error
        Exit Function
    End If
    If n = 0 Then
        Fibonacci = 0
        Exit Function
    End If

    Dim a, b, temp, i
    a = CLng(0)
    b = CLng(1)

    For i = 2 To n
        temp = a + b
        a = b
        b = temp
    Next
    Fibonacci = b
End Function

WScript.Echo "First 15 Fibonacci numbers:"
Dim result, i
result = ""
For i = 0 To 14
    result = result & Fibonacci(i) & " "
Next
WScript.Echo result
factorial:
' Function to calculate factorial N!
' Using Currency type for result to handle larger numbers than Long (up to 20!)
Function Factorial(n)
    If Not IsNumeric(n) Or n < 0 Then
        Factorial = -1 ' Indicate error
        Exit Function
    End If
    
    Dim result, i
    result = CCur(1) ' Initialize as Currency type
    For i = 2 To n
        result = result * i
    Next
    Factorial = result
End Function

WScript.Echo "Factorial of 5 is " & Factorial(5)
WScript.Echo "Factorial of 0 is " & Factorial(0)
WScript.Echo "Factorial of 20 is " & Factorial(20)
primeCheck:
' Function to check if a number is prime
Function IsPrime(n)
    If Not IsNumeric(n) Then
        IsPrime = False
        Exit Function
    End If

    If n <= 1 Then
        IsPrime = False
    ElseIf n <= 3 Then
        IsPrime = True
    ElseIf n Mod 2 = 0 Or n Mod 3 = 0 Then
        IsPrime = False
    Else
        Dim i, limit
        i = 5
        limit = Int(Sqr(n))
        IsPrime = True ' Assume prime until a divisor is found
        Do While i <= limit
            If n Mod i = 0 Or n Mod (i + 2) = 0 Then
                IsPrime = False
                Exit Do
            End If
            i = i + 6
        Loop
    End If
End Function

Dim nums, num
nums = Array(2, 3, 4, 17, 25, 29, 97)
For Each num In nums
    If IsPrime(num) Then
        WScript.Echo num & " is prime."
    Else
        WScript.Echo num & " is not prime."
    End If
Next
readFile:
Const ForReading = 1
Dim fso, f, ts, line
Dim filename
filename = "vbscript_read_test.txt"

Set fso = CreateObject("Scripting.FileSystemObject")

' Create a dummy file for reading
On Error Resume Next
Set ts = fso.CreateTextFile(filename, True)
If Err.Number <> 0 Then
    WScript.Echo "Error creating file: " & Err.Description
Else
    ts.WriteLine "First line from VBScript."
    ts.WriteLine "Second line."
    ts.Close
    WScript.Echo "Created dummy file: " & filename
End If
On Error GoTo 0

WScript.Echo vbCrLf & "--- Reading file '" & filename & "' ---"
If fso.FileExists(filename) Then
    Set f = fso.OpenTextFile(filename, ForReading)
    Do While Not f.AtEndOfStream
        line = f.ReadLine
        WScript.Echo "Read line: " & line
    Loop
    f.Close
Else
    WScript.Echo "Error: File not found."
End If

Set fso = Nothing
writeFile:
Const ForWriting = 2
Const ForAppending = 8
Dim fso, ts
Dim filename
filename = "output_vbscript.txt"

Set fso = CreateObject("Scripting.FileSystemObject")

' 1. Overwrite or create a new file
WScript.Echo "--- Writing (Overwrite) to '" & filename & "' ---"
Set ts = fso.OpenTextFile(filename, ForWriting, True)
ts.WriteLine "This line overwrites existing content."
ts.WriteLine "Written from a VBScript script."
ts.Close
WScript.Echo "Content written successfully."

' 2. Append to the existing file
WScript.Echo vbCrLf & "--- Appending to '" & filename & "' ---"
Set ts = fso.OpenTextFile(filename, ForAppending, True)
ts.WriteLine "This line was appended."
ts.Close
WScript.Echo "Content appended successfully."

Set fso = Nothing
fileExists:
Dim fso, fileToCheck, dirToCheck, nonExistent

Set fso = CreateObject("Scripting.FileSystemObject")

fileToCheck = "vbscript.json" ' Should exist
dirToCheck  = "." ' Current directory
nonExistent = "no_such_file.vbs"

WScript.Echo "--- Checking for files and folders ---"

If fso.FileExists(fileToCheck) Then
    WScript.Echo "'" & fileToCheck & "' is a file."
Else
    WScript.Echo "'" & fileToCheck & "' is NOT a file."
End If

If fso.FolderExists(dirToCheck) Then
    WScript.Echo "'" & dirToCheck & "' is a folder."
Else
    WScript.Echo "'" & dirToCheck & "' is NOT a folder."
End If

If fso.FileExists(nonExistent) Then
    WScript.Echo "'" & nonExistent & "' exists."
Else
    WScript.Echo "'" & nonExistent & "' does NOT exist."
End If

Set fso = Nothing
dateTime:
' VBScript Date and Time Functions

WScript.Echo "--- Current Date and Time ---"

' Get current date and time
WScript.Echo "Now: " & Now()

' Get just the date or time
WScript.Echo "Date: " & Date()
WScript.Echo "Time: " & Time()

WScript.Echo vbCrLf & "--- Components ---"
WScript.Echo "Year: " & Year(Now())
WScript.Echo "Month: " & Month(Now())
WScript.Echo "Day: " & Day(Now())
WScript.Echo "Hour: " & Hour(Now())
WScript.Echo "Minute: " & Minute(Now())
WScript.Echo "Second: " & Second(Now())
WScript.Echo "Weekday: " & WeekdayName(Weekday(Now()))

WScript.Echo vbCrLf & "--- Formatting ---"
WScript.Echo "Formatted (General): " & FormatDateTime(Now(), 0) ' vbGeneralDate
WScript.Echo "Formatted (Long Date): " & FormatDateTime(Now(), 1) ' vbLongDate
WScript.Echo "Formatted (Short Date): " & FormatDateTime(Now(), 2) ' vbShortDate
WScript.Echo "Formatted (Long Time): " & FormatDateTime(Now(), 3) ' vbLongTime
maxInArray:
' Function to find the maximum value in an array
Function MaxInArray(arr)
    If IsEmpty(arr) Or UBound(arr) < LBound(arr) Then
        MaxInArray = Null ' Return Null for empty array
        Exit Function
    End If

    Dim maxVal, element
    maxVal = arr(LBound(arr)) ' Initialize with first element
    For Each element In arr
        If element > maxVal Then
            maxVal = element
        End If
    Next
    MaxInArray = maxVal
End Function

Dim numbers
numbers = Array(10, 5, 42, 17, 8, 99, 32, -5, 0)
WScript.Echo "Array: " & Join(numbers, ", ")
WScript.Echo "Maximum value: " & MaxInArray(numbers)

Dim emptyArray()
' VBScript empty arrays cause errors with UBound, so check first
' WScript.Echo "Maximum (empty): " & MaxInArray(emptyArray)
